# Analysis functions

## Extracting parameters from model objects

model objects are non-tidy. for simulations, we usually want tidy inputs and outputs. so, we need to write analysis functions that receive tidy inputs and return tidy outputs.

this requires us to know how to access model parameters from their fitted objects.

this, again, involves writing the 'do stuff' part of the function first.

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#| include: false
# create dataset to be used/cleaned

# install the devtools and truffle packages if you don't have them already:
# library(devtools)
# install_github("ianhussey/truffle")
library(truffle)

dat_depression_rct <- 
  truffle_likert(study_design = "factorial_between2",
                 n_per_condition = 20,
                 factors  = "X1_latent",
                 prefixes = "X1_item",
                 alpha = .70,
                 n_items = 13,
                 n_levels = 4,
                 approx_d_between_groups = 0.60) |>
  snuffle_sum_scores("X1_", 
                     min = 1, 
                     max = 4, 
                     id_col = "id") |>
  select(id, condition, score = X1_sumscore) |>
  mutate(score = score - 13) |>
  truffle_demographics()
```

### Accessing model results

assign model results to object; use $ on model object to see its elements

```{r}

fit_hypthesis_test <- t.test(formula = score ~ condition,
                             var.equal = FALSE, # students t test: assumes equal variances
                             data = dat_depression_rct)

fit_hypthesis_test

fit_hypthesis_test$statistic
fit_hypthesis_test$parameter
fit_hypthesis_test$p.value
fit_hypthesis_test$estimate

```

Note that, despite the fact that a t-test is for estimating the differences in means between groups, R's t-test function does not return an esimate of the difference in means! we have to calculate it ourselves. [Uri Simonsohn recently pointed this out in a blog post](https://datacolada.org/132).

```{r}

as.numeric(fit_hypthesis_test$estimate[1] - fit_hypthesis_test$estimate[2])

```


exercise: tidy the result into a tibble

```{r}
# answer:
tibble(
  t = fit_hypthesis_test$statistic,
  df = fit_hypthesis_test$parameter,
  p = fit_hypthesis_test$p.value,
  m_diff = as.numeric(fit_hypthesis_test$estimate[1] - fit_hypthesis_test$estimate[2])
)

```


## Tests of means

### Independent t test

exercise: using the above, write a function to extract the t, df, p, and m_diff from a students t test. The inputs are only the tibble containing the data; it can assume score ~ group

```{r}



```

getting fancier - often won't be needed. use {{ column name }} to make the function more flexible.

```{r}



```

Because this extracting parameters such a common task, R libraries have been written to help us do it: [{parameters}](https://easystats.github.io/parameters/).

```{r}

library(parameters)

model_parameters(fit_hypthesis_test) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>% # use snake_case
  # we might still need to rename things for clarity
  rename(m_diff = difference,
         m_diff_ci_low = ci_low,
         m_diff_ci_high = ci_high)

```

extracting effect sizes

use the {effectsize} package where possible over alternatives, as its written by the same group who write {parameters} and plays nice with it and tidyverse

```{r}

library(effectsize)

fit_effect_size <- 
  cohens_d(score ~ condition,
           data = dat_depression_rct) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>% # use snake_case
  rename(cohens_d_ci_low = ci_low, cohens_d_ci_high = ci_high)

fit_effect_size

```

exercise: write a function that extracts the t, df, p, m_diff and its CIs, cohens d and its CIs, using {parameters} and {effectsize}

```{r}



```


#### Write more flexible functions

- students t vs Welches' t
- alternative
- different mu

### Dependent t test and cohen's d_rm

...

### (RM)ANOVA F-tests

afex::aov_ex is the gold standard for fitting (repeated measures) anovas in R using type III sum of squares, while avoiding the weird things that can happen with base-R's aov().

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(afex)

# 1. Correct Simulation
set.seed(123)
n_subjects <- 40

# Assign each ID to only ONE group (Between-subjects)
subjects <- data.frame(
  id = factor(1:n_subjects),
  group = rep(c("Treatment", "Control"), each = n_subjects/2)
)

# Expand only the Within-subjects factor (Time)
df_corrected <- subjects %>%
  group_by(id, group) %>%
  reframe(time = factor(c("Pre", "Post"))) %>%
  mutate(
    score = rnorm(n(), mean = 10, sd = 2) + 
            (time == "Post") * 2 + 
            (group == "Treatment" & time == "Post") * 3
  )

# 2. Fit the model again
model <- aov_ez(
  id = "id", 
  dv = "score", 
  data = df_corrected,
  between = "group", 
  within = "time"
)

# Extract parameters
parameters::model_parameters(model) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  janitor::clean_names() # use snake_case


# Partial Eta-Squared (Standard)
effectsize::eta_squared(model, partial = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  janitor::clean_names() # use snake_case

# Generalized Eta-Squared (Recommended for Mixed Designs)
effectsize::eta_squared(model, generalized = TRUE) %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  janitor::clean_names() # use snake_case

```

write a function to provide tidy results of an RM ANOVA for a 2X2 pre/post intervention/control design, its f values, p values, dfs, peta2 and geta2 effect sizes.

## Tests of correlation/covariance

### Bivariate correlations

...

### Regressions

...

## CFA/SEM

lavaan

## Test of other parameters (aka assumption tests)

- equal variances
- normality
- etc


## Pre-analysis functions 

- data transformation / outlier exclusions / etc
- hacking
- ...




## To add

-
